member.name: Shivani Raja
member.synopsis: Shivani Raja is the Conservative MP for Leicester East , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Visas: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what proportion of long term visa applications subject to a minimum service standard were decided within the relevant service standard by visa category since 1 January 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Visa processing times are published on the UKVI website at Visa decision waiting times: applications outside the UK - GOV.UK (www.gov.uk) and Visa decision waiting times: applications inside the UK - GOV.UK (www.gov.uk). UKVI are currently processing appl...

writtenQuestions.heading: Network Rail: Assets
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 24 March 2026 to Question 121192 on Network Rail: Assets, when Network Rail's next Delivery Plan update is expected to be published.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Network Rail expects to publish the Delivery Plan update document for 2026/27 in July 2026.

writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon. Member to the answer given on 29th June to Question 11460

writtenQuestions.heading: Railway Stations: Construction
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what lessons learned reviews have been undertaken by her Department into delayed railway station construction projects since 2020.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since 2020, the Department for Transport has undertaken a number of lessons‑learned reviews relevant to delayed railway station construction, including reviews of the sponsorship, governance, and assurance of major rail infrastructure programmes. The Depa...

writtenQuestions.heading: Active Travel: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether her Department requires independent verification of baseline cycling and pedestrian counts used to support applications for Active Travel England funding.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Following publication of the Devolution White Paper in 2024, the Government has consolidated most local transport funding streams into a single flexible fund that enables local areas to align investment with priorities and reduce burdens on accessing the ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Road Traffic Offences: Fixed Penalties and Prosecutions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many fixed penalty notices and prosecutions were issued for road traffic offences in England and Wales in each year since 2010, broken down by offence category.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport does not hold the information requested. Fixed penalty notices are a matter for the Home Office. The Home Office publishes statistics on fixed penalty notices and other outcomes for motoring offences as part of its Police powe...

writtenQuestions.heading: High Speed 2 Line: Tunnels
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, with reference to the HS2 six-monthly report to Parliament published in May 2026, what lessons learned and innovative construction methods were deployed in the completion of the north portal structure at the Ch
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Chiltern Tunnel North and South Portals have been specially engineered with perforated concrete hoods to prevent the possibility of sudden changes in air pressure and a resulting ‘sonic boom’ caused by trains entering and exiting the 10-mile-long tunn...

writtenQuestions.heading: Liverpool Street Station
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the potential impact on the Liverpool Street Station redevelopment scheme of changes in senior leadership at Platform4 and Network Rail Property since January 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Platform4, GBR Anglia & Network Rail Eastern region continue to work together on the scheme.The Liverpool Street Redevelopment scheme is being promoted by Platform 4 on a commercial basis – no government funding has been allocated. Any specific querie...

writtenQuestions.heading: Liverpool Street Station
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what estimate has been made of the total funding gap associated with the Liverpool Street Station redevelopment scheme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Liverpool Street Redevelopment scheme is being promoted by Platform 4 on a commercial basis – no government funding has been allocated. Any specific queries regarding this scheme should be directed to Platform 4 (info@platform4.com). Schemes of this s...

writtenQuestions.heading: Airspace
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many staff are assigned to delivery of the Lower Airspace Concept programme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The delivery of changes to lower terminal airspace, as part of the Airspace Modernisation Programme, is undertaken by the airports for three of the four Clusters; Scotland, Western and Manchester. The fourth cluster, London, will be undertaken by the UK A...

writtenQuestions.heading: Airspace
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what estimate she has made of the amount of fuel burn resulting from the time taken for Free Route Airspace implementation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Following the implementation of the Free Route Airspace over Scotland, NATS have calculated a predicted reduction of emissions by approximately 17,000 tonnes of CO₂e per year.

writtenQuestions.heading: Motor Vehicles: Software
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps his Department is taking to ensure the long-term competitiveness of the UK automotive industry in the context of increasing reliance on software-defined vehicle technologies.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is supporting the long-term competitiveness of the UK automotive industry by backing the technologies that will shape the next generation of vehicles. Our flagship DRIVE35 initiative is supporting research and development in zero emission ...

items.text: Name of donor: NXT Foundation Address of donor: 275, 276 Media House, Captain Gaur Marg, Sriniwaspuri, New Delhi, Delhi Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Attendance, participation, flights and accommodation at the NXT Conference. A thank you hamper was also provided with an estimated value of £200, value £4,961.91 Destination of visit: India Dates of visit: 9 March 2026 to 15 March 2026 Purpose of visit: Attended the NXT Conference in India to give a speech on Youth Engagement in Politics and take part in panel discussions on issues facing the global community. My Spouse accompanied me to care for our 6 month old baby. (Registered 25 March 2026; updated 7 April 2026)
